scope.summary: P-U2 adds inclusive from_line/to_line range fetch for oracle_get_source, pairs the range with oracle_search_source line numbers, and discloses the returned line range.
out_of_scope: No live Oracle source-range lane is claimed from this evidence.
No full-workspace gate is claimed from this shared dirty checkout.
This close does not alter oracle_search_source matching semantics; it only documents and exercises the handoff from search LINE values to oracle_get_source from_line/to_line.

entry_points.name: oracle_get_source range dispatch
entry_points.trace: env -u CARGO_TARGET_DIR cargo test -p oraclemcp get_source_reports_returned_line_range_and_refuses_outside_it -- --nocapture passed: from_line=40/to_line=48 returns range.returned 40..42, backwards ranges are InvalidArguments, and out-of-range requests suggest oracle_search_source.

entry_points.name: dictionary bind-shape guard
entry_points.trace: env -u CARGO_TARGET_DIR cargo test -p oraclemcp-db --test positional_bind_shape -- --nocapture passed: dictionary SQL does not reuse positional placeholders, including the source range SQL's one bind per placeholder occurrence.

entry_points.name: scoped crate checks
entry_points.trace: env -u CARGO_TARGET_DIR cargo check -p oraclemcp-db and env -u CARGO_TARGET_DIR cargo check -p oraclemcp both passed.

entry_points.name: tool-schema cross-reference
entry_points.trace: rg confirmed oracle_get_source exposes from_line/to_line in crates/oraclemcp/src/registry.rs and golden capability artifacts, with from_line instructing callers to pair with oracle_search_source's LINE result and oracle_search_source max_line_chars pointing back to oracle_get_source from_line/to_line for exact ranges.

limitations: Scoped local tests only; no hosted or full-workspace proof is claimed here.
The source SHA is the clean in-scope tree used for evidence generation, which already contained the previously landed P-U2 implementation.
